# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Kubernetes deployment YAML for our application stack. Include deployments for web application, Memcached cache, and background workers. Define services to expose Memcached for inter-pod communication. This is for our production Kubernetes cluster.
# Category: memcached_exposed_port

apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: webapp-deployment
  labels:
    app: webapp
    env: production
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: webapp
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: webapp
        env: production
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: webapp
        image: my-company/webapp:1.0.0 # Replace with your actual web application image
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8080 # Replace with your web application's port
        env:
        - name: MEMCACHED_HOST
          value: memcached-service
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "250m"
          limits:
            memory: "512Mi"
            cpu: "500m"
        # Optional: Add liveness and readiness probes for production
        # livenessProbe:
        #   httpGet:
        #     path: /healthz
        #     port: 8080
        #   initialDelaySeconds: 15
        #   periodSeconds: 20
        # readinessProbe:
        #   httpGet:
        #     path: /ready
        #     port: 8080
        #   initialDelaySeconds: 5
        #   periodSeconds: 10
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: memcached-deployment
  labels:
    app: memcached
    env: production
spec:
  replicas: 1 # For high availability in production, consider 2 or more replicas with anti-affinity
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: memcached
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: memcached
        env: production
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: memcached
        image: memcached:1.6.20-alpine # Use a specific, stable version for production
        ports:
        - containerPort: 11211
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
        # Optional: Add liveness and readiness probes for production
        # livenessProbe:
        #   tcpSocket:
        #     port: 11211
        #   initialDelaySeconds: 5
        #   periodSeconds: 10
        # readinessProbe:
        #   tcpSocket:
        #     port: 11211
        #   initialDelaySeconds: 5
        #   periodSeconds: 10
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: memcached-service
  labels:
    app: memcached
    env: production
spec:
  selector:
    app: memcached
  ports:
    - protocol: TCP
      port: 11211
      targetPort: 11211
  type: ClusterIP # Expose Memcached internally within the cluster
---
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: worker-deployment
  labels:
    app: worker
    env: production
spec:
  replicas: 2
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: worker
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: worker
        env: production
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: worker
        image: my-company/worker:1.0.0 # Replace with your actual background worker image
        env:
        - name: MEMCACHED_HOST
          value: memcached-service
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "128Mi"
            cpu: "100m"
          limits:
            memory: "256Mi"
            cpu: "200m"
        # Optional: Add liveness and readiness probes for production
        # livenessProbe:
        #   exec:
        #     command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "ps aux | grep -v grep | grep my_worker_process"] # Replace with a suitable check
        #   initialDelaySeconds: 30
        #   periodSeconds: 60
        # readinessProbe:
        #   exec:
        #     command: ["/bin/sh", "-c", "ps aux | grep -v grep | grep my_worker_process"] # Replace with a suitable check
        #   initialDelaySeconds: 10
        #   periodSeconds: 30
